description: Civilian Everyday Life Scenario Library — scenarios 1 through 20. Covers workplace stress, interpersonal dynamics, personal performance, daily frustrations, and goal-setting. Each scenario includes activating event, likely emotions, Four Blocks methodology fix, and rational redirection protocol for RAG ingestion.
source: NotebookLM cross-reference against My4Blocks methodology backbone

scenarios.activating_event: Feeling pressure to perform perfectly before an important work meeting or presentation.
scenarios.methodology_fix: Need for Approval and Anxiety Formula (AX = WI + AW + ICSI). The user is demanding perfect performance — a must belief that creates anticipatory anxiety.
scenarios.rational_redirection: I would prefer to perform well, but there is no law that I must be perfect. I am a Fallible Human Being. Use the Centering Breath to stop the Narrator's What If story and return to the present.

scenarios.activating_event: Receiving criticism from a manager or colleague that feels personal and unfair.
scenarios.methodology_fix: BS Equation (B = S). The user is treating criticism of their behavior as a verdict on their worth as a person.
scenarios.rational_redirection: Their criticism is about the behavior, not the Self. I rate the behavior — Behavior equals Behavior. I accept myself unconditionally regardless of their opinion.

scenarios.activating_event: Worrying about a conflict with a friend or family member and what might happen next.
scenarios.methodology_fix: Anxiety Formula — What If thinking (WI). The Narrator is projecting a future conflict scenario that has not yet occurred.
scenarios.rational_redirection: Staying Now. I will handle what arrives when it arrives. The conflict in my head is a story, not reality. I return to the Observer.

scenarios.activating_event: Feeling frustrated when a coworker behaves rudely or inconsiderately.
scenarios.methodology_fix: Anger Formula (A = ET + S). The user is demanding that a fallible coworker behave according to their personal standard.
scenarios.rational_redirection: They are acting from ignorance or their own disturbance. I would prefer they were more considerate, but I do not need them to be to remain okay.

scenarios.activating_event: Feeling overwhelmed when multiple problems or demands arrive at the same time.
scenarios.methodology_fix: I Can't Stand It (ICSI). The belief that the volume of simultaneous demands is unendurable.
scenarios.rational_redirection: I am standing this right now — which proves I can stand it. Focus on just this one task. One breath at a time. I am not required to solve everything at once.

scenarios.activating_event: Feeling guilty or ashamed after making a mistake at work.
scenarios.methodology_fix: BS Equation (B = S) and Shoulds/Musts/Demands. The user is rating the mistake as proof they are a bad or inadequate person.
scenarios.rational_redirection: The mistake should have happened because it did — the conditions for it were met. I am a Fallible Human Being. Behavior equals Behavior. I learn from it and move forward.

scenarios.activating_event: Anxiety after receiving unexpected medical news or awaiting test results.
scenarios.methodology_fix: Anxiety Formula — What If (WI) and Awfulizing (AW). The Narrator is constructing worst-case medical scenarios that do not yet exist.
scenarios.rational_redirection: Staying Now. I will handle the facts when they arrive. The horror story in my head is not real. Use the Centering Breath and return to this moment.

scenarios.activating_event: Feeling disrespected or dismissed during a conversation with someone important to them.
scenarios.methodology_fix: Egocentric Thinking (ET) and Need for Approval. The user is demanding acknowledgment and respect from another fallible human being.
scenarios.rational_redirection: I would prefer to be heard and respected, but I do not need their acknowledgment to maintain my worth. Their dismissal is their limitation, not a verdict on my value.

scenarios.activating_event: Feeling hopeless when a personal goal feels increasingly out of reach.
scenarios.methodology_fix: Depression Formula (D = H1 + H2 + N) — Pseudo-Need. The user believes they need this goal to be fulfilled to be happy or worthy.
scenarios.rational_redirection: I want this goal, but I do not need it to function or to have worth. I choose to be an Observer of my disappointment rather than a victim of it.

scenarios.activating_event: Feeling intense pressure before a high-stakes performance, exam, or evaluation.
scenarios.methodology_fix: Shoulds/Musts/Demands — the user is applying a must to their own performance that generates performance anxiety.
scenarios.rational_redirection: I would prefer to perform well, but I am a Fallible Human Being. Total Acceptance of the current moment equals zero disturbance. Staying Now.

scenarios.activating_event: Becoming angry in traffic when other drivers behave recklessly or inconsiderately.
scenarios.methodology_fix: Anger Formula (A = ET + S). The user is demanding that other drivers comply with their personal standard of behavior.
scenarios.rational_redirection: That driver exists because the conditions for them exist. They are acting from ignorance or their own disturbance. Replace 'This is awful' with 'This is inconvenient.' I prefer better drivers, I do not need them.

scenarios.activating_event: Feeling inadequate when comparing their life or achievements to others on social media.
scenarios.methodology_fix: Rating. The user is assigning their worth based on an external comparison against curated social media content.
scenarios.rational_redirection: Comparison is Rating. Their presentation is their reality — not a standard you must meet. I accept my current reality as it is and practice Unconditional Self-Acceptance.

scenarios.activating_event: Feeling envious or resentful when a friend or colleague achieves something they have been working toward.
scenarios.methodology_fix: Rating and Egocentric Thinking. The user is treating another person's success as evidence of their own inadequacy — comparing life paths that are not comparable.
scenarios.rational_redirection: Their success is their reality. My life is my reality. Comparison is Rating. I accept where I am and focus on what I can do in this moment.

scenarios.activating_event: Feeling guilt about a past mistake that affected an important relationship.
scenarios.methodology_fix: BS Equation and Shoulds. The user is rating the mistake as proof of their failure as a person in the relationship — applying an irrational should to unalterable past behavior.
scenarios.rational_redirection: The mistake should have happened because it did — the conditions were met. Behavior equals Behavior. I am not a failure as a person. I repair what I can and refuse to punish myself for being fallible.

scenarios.activating_event: Feeling overwhelmed by a long list of responsibilities with no clear starting point.
scenarios.methodology_fix: I Can't Stand It (ICSI) and SOS Method. The user is telling themselves the totality of the list is unmanageable — treating the whole as the immediate task.
scenarios.rational_redirection: Focus on just this one task. Use the SOS Method to identify what you are telling yourself about the situation. I am standing it one breath at a time.

scenarios.activating_event: Feeling hopeless after a series of setbacks or failed attempts toward a goal.
scenarios.methodology_fix: Depression Formula — Hopelessness (H1) and Pseudo-Need (N). The user believes they need the goal to be achieved to experience happiness or worth.
scenarios.rational_redirection: I want success, but I do not need it to function or be okay. The setbacks happened because conditions were met. I choose to be an Observer of my disappointment rather than a victim of it.

scenarios.activating_event: Feeling angered when someone repeatedly fails to follow through on commitments.
scenarios.methodology_fix: Anger Formula (A = ET + S). The user is demanding that another fallible human being behave reliably according to their standard.
scenarios.rational_redirection: They are acting from ignorance or their own limitations. I would prefer they followed through, but demanding it only disturbs me. I address the behavior and accept the fallibility.

scenarios.activating_event: Feeling worthless after receiving repeated negative feedback or rejection.
scenarios.methodology_fix: BS Equation and Depression Formula. The user is treating repeated rejection as a global rating of their worth as a person.
scenarios.rational_redirection: I am rating my behavior — Behavior equals Behavior. I accept myself unconditionally regardless of their feedback. Rejection is an event, not a verdict.

scenarios.activating_event: Feeling anxious and uncertain before starting something new — a job, relationship, or major life change.
scenarios.methodology_fix: Fear of Uncertainty and What If thinking (WI). The Narrator is generating worst-case projections about a future that has not yet unfolded.
scenarios.rational_redirection: Uncertainty is a fact of life. I cannot predict the future. Staying Now. I make the best decision I can as a Fallible Human Being and trust my ability to handle what arrives.

scenarios.activating_event: Feeling stuck and unfulfilled when life does not match personal expectations or dreams.
scenarios.methodology_fix: Depression Formula — Pseudo-Need (N) and Hopelessness (H1). The user believes they need their life to match a specific vision to be happy or have worth.
scenarios.rational_redirection: This is a Want, not a Need for my survival or worth. The life I have is the life whose conditions were met. I choose to be an Observer of my disappointment and focus on what I can build from where I am.

implementation_notes.library_context: Civilian Scenario Library — everyday life scenarios for non-first-responder users. Covers workplace, interpersonal, performance, daily frustration, and goal-setting contexts.
implementation_notes.source_tool: NotebookLM — cross-referenced against My4Blocks methodology backbone PDF
implementation_notes.notebooklm_note: NotebookLM returned grouped output with Rational Replacement Table for this batch. Individual scenarios were expanded by Claude from the grouped protocols and cross-reference table.
implementation_notes.enhanced_fields: Includes methodology_fix and rational_redirection fields consistent with FR081-FR100 and CIVILIAN_SCENARIO_LIBRARY_61_80 format
implementation_notes.routing_note: Match scenario context to user input → identify stage → apply methodology fix → deliver rational redirection through civilian personality layer

stage_routing.pre_event: Route to anticipatory anxiety or pre-event behavior responses
stage_routing.during_event: Route to active coping — SOS method, centering breath, just this task
stage_routing.post_event: Route to processing responses — mental contamination protocol, formula application, rational redirection
